theme: Discover the transformation a dataset ignores
title: The Flat Direction: Discovering an Affine Symmetry Generator
strategies: trivial: declare a fixed pure-translation generator along +x, ignoring the data entirely -> reproduces the checker's own baseline construction, Ratio ~0.1

greedy: pool every point from every stencil group into one global least-squares linear regression label~b0+b1*x+b2*y, and declare the null direction (perpendicular to the fitted gradient) as a universal translation-invariance direction. Correct regime for translation-type instances, but has no way to represent a center, so it is systematically wrong (and, since the fit is global, curvature-biased) whenever the true symmetry is a rotation or scaling about a hidden point -- exactly the trap on >=3 of the 10 cases

strong: use the input's own local finite-difference stencils to estimate the label's GRADIENT at each anchor. A rotation's gradient is radial (points at the center) and a scaling's gradient is tangential (perpendicular to the radius); either way, a simple direction derived from the gradient traces a LINE through the true center. Intersecting these lines (least-squares) across many anchors recovers the exact center in closed form, without ever fitting the label surface -- and lets the declared generator's flow agree with the true label far outside the training slab. Falls back to averaged-gradient translation when no line concurrency is found

headroom above strong: robustify the center solve against finite-difference/curvature bias (e.g. weighted line intersection, or a second-order correction using the group's own quadratic stencil residual) to push the surviving error toward the residual discretization floor
